text: <sup>{The Book of}</sup> <sup>{Micah}</sup> d <sup>{God's Coming Judgment}</sup> d This message from the Lord came to Micah <sup>{The Heb. name Micah means Who is like the Lord ?}</sup> of Moresheth during the reigns of <sup>{Lit. days}</sup> the Judean kings Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah concerning the vision he saw about Samaria and Jerusalem:
"Listen, people! All of you! Earth! Pay attention, and all you inhabitants of it! May the Lord God be a witness against you the Lord from his holy Temple.
Look here! The Lord is coming from his place! He will come down and will trample down the high places <sup>{I.e. the sites of idol worship}</sup> throughout the land.
The mountains will melt under him and the valleys will split apart, like wax in the presence of fire and like water gushing down a steep incline.

All this comes about due to the transgression of Jacob, and due to the sins of the house of Israel. What is Jacob's sin? It's Samaria, isn't it? And what's Judah's high place? <sup>{I.e. the sites of idol worship}</sup> It's Jerusalem, isn't it?
"So I will turn Samaria into a mound of dirt in a field, a place to plant vineyards. And I will dump her building stones into the valley, uncovering her foundation.

All of her carved images will be crushed to pieces, all the earnings of her prostitution will be burned up, and I will destroy all her idols; because she collected the wages of prostitution, and to the wages of prostitution they will return."
<sup>{The Coming Destruction}</sup> d "Therefore I will cry out and grieve loudly; I will walk around stripped and naked. I will cry out like a jackal and mourn like a company of ostriches.
For Samaria's <sup>{Lit. her}</sup> injury is fatal, reaching all the way to Judah, extending even to the gate of my people to Jerusalem."
"Don't discuss it in Gath! <sup>{A city in Philistia}</sup> Don't cry bitterly in Akim! <sup>{So LXX; MT reads Don't cry at all}</sup> Roll in the ashes, Beth-leaphrah!
Run away, you residents of Shaphir, displaying your shameful nakedness. Don't come out, you residents of Zaanan! <sup>{A city of Judah}</sup> Your firm standing will disappear as Beth-ezel mourns.
Even though the inhabitants of Maroth long for success, nevertheless evil descended from the Lord to the gate of Jerusalem.
"You inhabitants of Lachish, harness your chariot to your swiftest steed the daughter of Zion has begun to sin because within you the transgressions of Israel were revealed.
Therefore give your gifts to Moresheth-gath; that is, the houses of Achzib as a deceitful symbol <sup>{Or a lie}</sup> to the kings of Israel.
Nevertheless, I will deliver an heir to you, inhabitants of Mareshah to Adullam the glory of Israel will come.
"Shave your head and cut off your locks as you mourn your beloved children. Make yourself bald as an eagle, because they will go from you into exile!"

<sup>{God's Warning to His People}</sup> d "Woe to those who are crafting iniquity, planning evil well into the night! <sup>{Lit. evil upon their beds}</sup> When morning's light comes, they carry out their plans <sup>{The Heb. lacks their plans}</sup> because they have the power to do so.
They covet fields and seize them; they covet <sup>{The Heb. lacks they covet}</sup> houses, and grab them, too. They harass the valiant man, along with his household, an individual and his estate.

"Therefore this is what the Lord says, "I'm crafting evil against this family, from which you can't escape. <sup>{Lit. can't remove your necks}</sup> You won't strut around arrogantly, because the times are evil.'

"When this happens, <sup>{Lit. In that day}</sup> someone will compose a proverb about you, lamenting sorrowfully, «We are completely ruined! He has given my people's heritage to others. <sup>{The Heb. lacks to others}</sup> How he has removed it from me, dividing up our fields!'
"This is why there will not be left even a single person to settle boundary disputes <sup>{Lit. to stretch out a measuring line}</sup> in the Lord's community.
To those who speak out, they will declare, «Don't prophesy to anyone!'so their shame won't go away.
"It is said, house of Jacob, «The Spirit of the Lord is limited, if he acts this way, is he not? '"But my words benefit those who live righteously, do they not?
Lately my people have acted like an enemy you strip travelers who thought they were <sup>{The Heb. lacks thought they were}</sup> as secure as those who return from war.
You have evicted the wives of my people from their dream homes; you have removed my majesty from their children permanently.
"Get up and go, because there's no rest for you here! Since everything <sup>{Lit. it}</sup> is polluted, it can only cause destruction, even heavy destruction.
Suppose a man who keeps company with a deceiving spirit prophesies like this: «Drink wine and strong drink!'Won't the people accept him as a prophet?"

<sup>{The Coming Judgment}</sup> d "Jacob, how I will surely gather all of you! How I will gather the survivors of Israel! I will gather them together like sheep in a pen, <sup>{Or sheep of Bozrah}</sup> like the flock in the midst of the sheepfold. There will be a great commotion because of all <sup>{The Heb. lacks all}</sup> the people.

God will stand up and break through <sup>{Lit. The one who breaks through will arise}</sup> in their presence. Then they will pass through the gate, going out by it. Their king will pass in front of them with the Lord at their head."
"He will say, «Listen, you leaders of Jacob, you officials of the house of Israel! You should know justice, should you not?
you who despise good and love evil, who tear off the skin of my people, <sup>{The Heb. lacks of my people}</sup> along with the flesh from their bones.

You eat the flesh of my people, flaying their skin from them. You break their bones, chopping them in pieces like meat <sup>{The Heb. lacks meat}</sup> for a pot, like meat destined for a soup kettle.'
"Then they will cry to the Lord , but he will not listen to them. In fact, he will hide his face from them at that time, because they were so wicked in what they were doing."

<sup>{God's Judgment against False Prophets}</sup> d "This is what the Lord says about the prophets who are causing my people to go astray, who are calling out «Peace'when they're being fed, <sup>{Lit. who bite with their teeth, crying out "Peace"}</sup> but who declare war against those who won't feed them: <sup>{Lit. against him who puts nothing in their mouths}</sup>
«You will have nights without visions, and darkness without prophecy. The sun will set on the prophets, and the day will darken for them.
Those who see visions will be put to shame, and the diviners will be disgraced every one of them they will cover their faces, <sup>{Lit. beard}</sup> because there will be no answer from God.»"

<sup>{The Message of God's Prophet}</sup> d "As for me, I am truly filled with power by the Spirit of the Lord , filled <sup>{The Heb. lacks filled}</sup> with judgment and power to announce to Jacob his transgression, and to Israel his sin.

Please listen to this, you leaders of the house of Jacob, you officials of the house of Israel, you who hate administering justice, who pervert the very meaning of <sup>{Lit. pervert all}</sup> equity,
who are building up Zion by means of bloodshed, and Jerusalem by means of iniquity.

Her leaders judge for the money, her priests teach only when they're paid , and her prophets prophesy for cash. Even so, don't they all rely on the Lord as they ask, «The Lord is among us, is he not? Nothing bad can possibly happen to us!'
"Therefore, because of you, Zion will be plowed up like a field, and Jerusalem will become heaps of rubble, and the Temple Mount like a forest high place."

<sup>{The Future Reign of God}</sup> d "But in the last days it will come about that the Temple Mount of the Lord will be firmly set as the leading mountain. It will be exalted above its surrounding <sup>{The Heb. lacks surrounding}</sup> hills, and people will stream toward it.

Many nations will approach and say, «Come, let's go up to the mountain of the Lord , and to the Temple of the God of Jacob. He will teach us about his ways, and we will walk according to his directions. '"Indeed, the Law will proceed from Zion, and the message of the Lord from Jerusalem.

And he will judge among many people, rebuking strong nations far away; and they will reshape their swords as plowshares and their spears as pruning hooks. No nation will threaten another, <sup>{Lit. A nation will not lift up a sword against a nation}</sup> nor will they train for war anymore.

Instead, each man will sit in the shade of <sup>{Lit. sit under}</sup> his grape vines and beneath the shade of <sup>{The Heb. lacks the shade of}</sup> his fig tree," since the <sup>{Lit. the mouth of the}</sup> Lord of the Heavenly Armies has spoken.
"Because all of the people will walk, each person in the name of his God, and we will walk in the name of the Lord our God forever and ever.
"At that time,"declares the Lord , "I will assemble the lame; and I will gather those whom I have scattered, along with those whom I have afflicted.
I will transform the lame into survivors, and those who were scattered into a strong nation; and the Lord will reign over them in Mount Zion, now and forever."

<sup>{Zion's Captivity}</sup> d "And you, watchtower of the flock, you stronghold of the daughter of Zion, it will happen even to you: The former dominion, even the kingdom of the daughter of Jerusalem, will come.
Why are you crying so loud now? There's no king among you, is there? Perhaps your advisor has died? For pain has overtaken you like a woman in labor.

Be in pain! Be in labor, you daughter of Zion, like a woman about to give birth, because now you will depart from the city, living in the open fields. To Babylon you will go. There you will be delivered, there the Lord will rescue you from the power <sup>{Lit. hand}</sup> of your enemies."
<sup>{The Nations Despise Zion}</sup> d "Now many nations have gathered against you, saying, «Let her be defiled,'and «Let's look down on Zion.'
But they don't know the thoughts of the Lord , and they don't understand his tactics, for he will gather them like harvested grain to his threshing floor.

Get up and smash <sup>{Lit. thresh}</sup> them to pieces, daughter of Zion, because I will make your horn like iron and your hooves like bronze! And you will beat many people to pieces, and I will consecrate their dishonest <sup>{The Heb. lacks dishonest}</sup> gain to the Lord and their assets to the Lord of the entire earth."

<sup>{5:1 is 4:14 in MT}</sup> "Now marshal yourselves as troops. <sup>{Lit. yourselves, you daughter of mobs}</sup> He has laid siege to us. They will strike the judge <sup>{Or ruler}</sup> of Israel on the cheek with a rod."

<sup>{The Ruler from Bethlehem}</sup> d <sup>{5:2 is 5:1 in MT, 5:3 is 5:2 in MT, and so throughout the chapter}</sup> "As for you, Bethlehem of Ephrathah, even though you remain least among the clans <sup>{Or thousands}</sup> of Judah, nevertheless, the one who rules in Israel for me will emerge from you. His existence has been <sup>{The Heb. lacks has been}</sup> from antiquity, even from eternity.
Therefore that ruler <sup>{Lit. Therefore he}</sup> will abandon them until the woman in labor gives birth. Then the rest of his countrymen will return to the Israelis."

"Then he will take his stand, shepherding by means of the strength of the Lord , by the power <sup>{Or majesty}</sup> of the name of the Lord his God. And they will be firmly <sup>{The Heb. lacks firmly}</sup> established; indeed, from then on he will become great to the ends of the earth.

And he will be our peace." <sup>{God's Judgment on Assyria}</sup> d"When the Assyrian invades our land, trampling through our palaces, we will raise up seven shepherds against him, even eight significant men.

The shepherds <sup>{Lit. And they}</sup> will devastate the land of Assyria with the sword, along with the entrances to the land of Nimrod. "This is how he will vanquish <sup>{Lit. will deliver from}</sup> Assyria when he invades our land, trampling within our borders:
The survivors of Jacob will live among many peoples, as dew from the Lord , as showers upon the grass. They will look to no one, and will place no hope in human beings.
The survivors of Jacob will live among the nations; they will live among many peoples, like a lion among flocks of sheep, who, if he passes through, will trample and tear down with no one to deliver.
You will turn your power <sup>{Lit. hand}</sup> against your adversaries, and all of your enemies will be cut down."
<sup>{God Removes Idolatry}</sup> d "It will come about at that time,"declares the Lord , "I will tear away your horses from you, and I will destroy your chariots.
I will cut off the cities of your land, and I will tear down all of your fortifications.
I will render your witchcraft powerless, <sup>{Lit. will cut witchcraft out of your hands}</sup> and mediums will no longer exist among you.
I will separate you from your carved images and sacred pillars, and you no longer will worship what you've made with your hands.
I will uproot your cultic gods <sup>{Heb. Asherim}</sup> from you, and I will tear down your cities.
I will execute vengeance, anger, and fury on the nations who do not obey."
<sup>{The Lord's Indictment against Israel}</sup> d Please hear what the Lord says: "Get up and make your case before the mountains, and let the hills listen to your voice.

Listen, you mountains, to the Lord's argument! Listen, you <sup>{The Heb. lacks Listen, you}</sup> strong foundations of the earth, because the Lord has a dispute with his people, and he will set out his case before Israel.
"My people, what have I done to you, and how have I offended you? Answer me!
For I brought you up from the land of Egypt, and ransomed you from the house of slavery, sending Moses, Aaron, and Miriam into your presence.
"My people, recall how king Balak of Moab deliberated, and how Beor's son Balaam counseled him from Shittim to Gilgal, so that you may know the righteousness of the Lord ."

<sup>{The Nature of True Righteousness}</sup> d How am I to present myself in the Lord's presence and bow in the presence of the High God? Should I present myself with burnt offerings, with year-old calves?

Will the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams, or with endless <sup>{Lit. with ten thousand}</sup> rivers of oil? Am I to give my firstborn to pay for <sup>{The Heb. lacks to pay for}</sup> my rebellion, the fruit of my body in exchange for <sup>{The Heb. lacks in exchange for}</sup> my soul?

He has made it clear to you, mortal man, what is good and what the Lord is requiring from you to act with justice, to treasure the Lord's <sup>{The Heb. lacks the Lord's}</sup> gracious love, and to walk humbly in the company of your God.
<sup>{A Call to Honest Business Practices}</sup> d The voice of the Lord cries out to the city wisdom fears your name: "Heed the rod, and the one who prepared it!
Are there still wicked treasures in the house of the wicked, along with deceitful and abominable measuring standards? <sup>{Lit. with short, detestable ephahs}</sup>
Will I tolerate those who maintain deceptive standards <sup>{Lit. maintain evil balances}</sup> and who use deceitful weights in their business? <sup>{The Heb. lacks in their business}</sup>
Her rich people are filled with violence, and her inhabitants tell lies their tongues speak deceitfully!
"Therefore I will make you ill when I attack you; I will bring you to ruin because of your offenses.
You'll eat, but you won't have enough; and hunger will be common among you. You'll horde things, but you won't save them, and what you preserve I'll give over to the sword.
You'll plant, but you won't reap. You'll crush the olive harvest, but you'll have no oil to anoint yourself. You'll tread out the grapes, but you'll never drink wine.

You keep Omri's <sup>{I.e. King Omri of Israel, father of Ahab (cf. 2Kings 8:26; 2Chr 22:2)}</sup> statutes and observe the customs of the house of Ahab. Because you live according to their advice, I'll make you desolate and turn your <sup>{Lit. its}</sup> inhabitants into an object of scorn. Therefore you will bear the shame of my people."
<sup>{The Evil Behavior of the People}</sup> d Poor me! I feel like those who harvest summer fruit, or like those who pick grapes there are no clusters to eat or any fresh fruit that I want.

The faithful have died off, and there is not one upright human being in the land. They all stalk one another <sup>{The Heb. lacks one another}</sup> with lethal intent, a man will even hunt his own brother with a net.
And speaking of evil, they practice it eagerly with both hands! Both leader and judge demand a bribe, the famous confess their perverted desires, and they scheme together.

The best of them is like a thorn, and their most upright like a hedge of thorns. The day announced by <sup>{Lit. day of}</sup> your watchmen and by your own calculations approaches. Now it's your time to be <sup>{The Heb. lacks time to be}</sup> confused!
Don't trust your friends, don't confide in a companion, watch what you say to your wife. <sup>{Lit. her who lies near your bosom}</sup>

The son disrespects his father, the daughter rebels against her mother, the daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law, and a man's enemies are the people of his own house. <sup>{Cf. Matt 10:34-36}</sup>
<sup>{Micah Looks to God}</sup> d But as for me, I will look to the Lord ; I will wait for the God who will deliver me. My God will hear me.
Don't be glad on my account, my enemy. When I fall, I'll get up. Though I sit in darkness, the Lord is a light for me.

I will endure the Lord's anger since I have sinned against him until he takes over my defense, administers justice on my behalf, and brings me out to the light, where I will gaze on his righteousness.

Then my enemy will observe it, and shame will engulf the ones <sup>{Lit. will bury her}</sup> who asked me, «Where is the Lord your God? 'My own eyes will see them, <sup>{Lit. her}</sup> they <sup>{Lit. she}</sup> will be trampled on like mud in the streets.

<sup>{A Word of Restoration}</sup> d When the time comes <sup>{Lit. In the day}</sup> for rebuilding your walls, that time <sup>{Lit. in the day}</sup> will surely be extended. <sup>{Or that day the decree will be removed far away}</sup>

At that time <sup>{Lit. At that day}</sup> armies <sup>{Lit. they}</sup> will invade you from Assyria, from Egyptian cities to the Euphrates <sup>{The Heb. lacks Euphrates}</sup> River, from sea to sea and from mountain to mountain.
The land will become desolate because of its inhabitants, and as a result of their behavior.
Use your rod to shepherd your people, the flock that belongs to you, that lives alone in the forest of Carmel. Let them find pasture in Bashan and Gilead, as they did long ago.
As I did when <sup>{Lit. As in the days}</sup> you came out of the land of Egypt, I will show you <sup>{Lit. him}</sup> awesome things.
The nations will look on and will be ashamed in spite of all their power; they will cup their hands over their mouths, and their ears will be deaf.
They will lick the dust like a serpent; they will crawl from their strongholds like snakes. They will fear the Lord our God. They will be terrified because of you.

<sup>{Who is like God?}</sup> d Is there any God like you, forgiving iniquity, passing over transgressions by the survivors who are your <sup>{Lit. his}</sup> heritage? <sup>{I.e. God's people}</sup> He is not angry forever, because he delights in gracious love.
He will again show us compassion; he will subdue our iniquities. You will hurl all their sins into the deepest sea.
You will remain true to Jacob, and merciful to Abraham, as you promised our ancestors long ago.
